methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kvertus is a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Yaroslav Filimonov and Andrii Znaichenko, currently raising capital with approximately $130M deployed to date. The valuation universe consists of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI—drawn from a broader population of 21 Defense & Security peers.

Peer-median valuation was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Defense & Security cohort. The dataset contained only one adjacent-stage peer and zero same-stage peers meeting similarity thresholds, falling below the minimum of three required datapoints for reliable median triangulation. Broader sector median methodology was rejected as methodologically misleading given the heterogeneity of business models, maturity, and revenue scales across Defense & Security.

Given the constraints, alternative valuation approaches—DCF, revenue multiple analysis on the identified public and private comp set, or transaction precedent—would be necessary to establish a defensible range. The key uncertainty remains the absence of tight peer clustering at Kvertus's specific stage and scale, which limits direct market-based anchoring. Any valuation conclusion should explicitly acknowledge this methodological limitation and rely on fundamental metrics (growth rate, unit economics, contract pipeline) rather than statistical peer centering.
